questions.text: According to ISO 27001:2022, which clause specifically requires the organization to define and apply an information security risk assessment process?
options: D. Clause 10.1
B. Clause 8.2
A. Clause 6.1.2
C. Clause 9.1
questions.justification_correct: Clause 6.1.2 of ISO 27001:2022 explicitly mandates that organizations shall define and apply an information security risk assessment process, establishing risk acceptance criteria and ensuring repeatable results. This forms the foundation of the risk management framework.
questions.justification_wrong: Clause 8.2 addresses risk assessment execution, Clause 9.1 covers monitoring and measurement, and Clause 10.1 relates to nonconformity and corrective action. These clauses do not establish the fundamental requirement for defining the risk assessment process itself.

questions.text: In ISO 27005:2022, what is the primary purpose of asset identification in the risk assessment process?
options: A. To determine the financial value of organizational resources
D. To assign ownership responsibilities exclusively
C. To create an inventory for audit purposes only
B. To establish the scope and boundaries for risk assessment and identify what needs protection
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 Section 6.3 specifies that asset identification establishes the scope and boundaries of risk assessment, determining what needs protection based on business value and importance. This enables subsequent threat and vulnerability analysis aligned with organizational context.
questions.justification_wrong: While financial valuation, inventory management, and ownership assignment may be outcomes, they are not the primary purpose. Asset identification fundamentally defines what requires protection within the ISMS scope, enabling comprehensive risk assessment as per ISO 27005:2022 methodology.

questions.text: According to ISO 27005:2022, which factor is NOT typically considered when analyzing vulnerabilities during risk assessment?
options: A. Weakness in organizational procedures
D. Human factors and awareness gaps
B. Market competition factors
C. Technical configuration flaws
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 Section 6.5 defines vulnerabilities as weaknesses that can be exploited by threats. Market competition is a business strategic concern, not an information security vulnerability. Vulnerability analysis focuses on exploitable weaknesses in assets, controls, processes, or human factors.
questions.justification_wrong: Organizational procedure weaknesses, technical configuration flaws, and human factor gaps are all recognized vulnerability categories in ISO 27005:2022. These represent exploitable weaknesses that threaten information asset confidentiality, integrity, or availability and must be analyzed systematically.

questions.text: When establishing the Risk Treatment Plan (RTP) per ISO 27001:2022 Clause 6.1.3, which element is mandatory to include?
options: D. Marketing communication strategies
B. Third-party insurance policy details
C. Detailed penetration testing schedules
A. Information security risk owners responsible for managing identified risks
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 6.1.3(e) explicitly requires that risk treatment plans include information on risk owners. Risk ownership ensures accountability for implementing controls and managing residual risks. This is a mandatory documented information requirement for the RTP.
questions.justification_wrong: While insurance, testing schedules, and communications may support risk treatment, they are not mandatory RTP elements per ISO 27001:2022. The standard specifically requires risk owners, treatment actions, required resources, responsibilities, completion dates, and evaluation methods as core RTP components.

questions.text: According to ISO 27005:2022, residual risk is best defined as:
options: C. Risk that has been completely eliminated through controls
D. Risk transferred entirely to third-party service providers
B. Risk identified but not yet analyzed during the assessment process
A. Risk remaining after risk treatment measures have been implemented
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 Section 6.8 defines residual risk as the risk remaining after risk treatment. Even with controls implemented, some risk persists and must be formally accepted by risk owners if within acceptable criteria, or require additional treatment per ISO 27001:2022 Clause 6.1.3(d).
questions.justification_wrong: Unanalyzed risk is part of initial assessment, not residual risk. Complete elimination is rarely achievable in practice, and even transferred risks retain residual elements as organizations remain ultimately accountable. Residual risk specifically represents the remaining exposure post-treatment implementation.

questions.text: During asset identification according to ISO 27005:2022, which element is MOST critical to establish before conducting risk assessment?
options: D. Backup frequency for digital assets
A. Asset owners and their accountability boundaries
C. Purchase date and depreciation schedule
B. Physical location of all IT equipment
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 Clause 6.2.2 emphasizes identifying asset owners and accountability as fundamental prerequisites. Ownership establishes responsibility for protection, valuation, and treatment decisions. Without defined ownership, subsequent risk assessment activities lack proper governance structure and decision-making authority for implementing controls.
questions.justification_wrong: Physical location, purchase dates, and backup schedules are operational details that support asset management but are not prerequisites for risk assessment. ISO 27005:2022 prioritizes ownership and accountability establishment before detailed operational characteristics, as these determine who assesses and treats risks.

questions.text: When conducting threat analysis per ISO 27005:2022, which approach correctly identifies threat sources?
options: B. Consider human, environmental, and technical sources systematically
C. Prioritize only threats with historical incidents
D. Limit analysis to threats listed in Annex A
A. Focus exclusively on external malicious actors
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 Clause 6.3.2 requires comprehensive threat identification across all source categories: human (accidental/deliberate), environmental (natural phenomena), and technical (system failures). Systematic consideration ensures completeness and prevents overlooking emerging or atypical threats that could exploit vulnerabilities across the organization's asset inventory.
questions.justification_wrong: Limiting threat sources to external actors, historical incidents, or Annex A controls creates incomplete risk profiles. ISO 27005:2022 mandates holistic threat identification regardless of origin or precedent. Environmental and accidental human threats often represent significant risks that systematic analysis must capture comprehensively.

questions.text: In vulnerability assessment under ISO 27005:2022, what distinguishes a vulnerability from a weakness?
options: B. Vulnerabilities can be exploited by threats; weaknesses cannot
A. Vulnerabilities are always technical; weaknesses are procedural
C. Vulnerabilities exist only in software systems
D. No distinction exists; terms are interchangeable
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 Clause 6.3.3 defines vulnerabilities as weaknesses that can be exploited by threats. The exploitability criterion is decisive. A weakness becomes a vulnerability when a corresponding threat exists capable of exploitation. This distinction drives risk assessment, as unexploitable weaknesses pose no immediate risk.
questions.justification_wrong: Vulnerabilities span technical, physical, and organizational domains per ISO 27005:2022. The exploitability by threats, not the domain or system type, determines vulnerability classification. Treating terms interchangeably undermines precise risk analysis required by the standard for accurate risk level determination.

questions.text: According to ISO 27005:2022, what is the PRIMARY purpose of establishing risk evaluation criteria before risk analysis?
options: D. To eliminate the need for management involvement
C. To assign blame for identified vulnerabilities
B. To calculate the financial impact of all incidents
A. To determine which risks require treatment decisions
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 Clause 6.4 establishes risk evaluation criteria as decision thresholds determining acceptable risk levels and treatment priorities. Criteria provide consistent, objective baselines for comparing risks against organizational risk appetite, enabling systematic decisions on which risks need treatment versus acceptance.
questions.justification_wrong: While financial impact may inform criteria, ISO 27005:2022 requires broader considerations including strategic, compliance, and reputational factors. Criteria facilitate management decisions, not replace them. Blame assignment contradicts the standard's improvement-focused approach. Criteria enable informed risk treatment decisions per organizational context.

questions.text: In risk treatment planning per ISO 27005:2022, when is risk acceptance appropriate without additional controls?
options: C. When similar organizations accept comparable risks
A. When residual risk falls within defined acceptable risk criteria after evaluation
D. When IT department lacks resources for implementation
B. When budget constraints prevent control implementation
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 Clause 6.5.3 permits risk acceptance when residual risk meets pre-established acceptable criteria and receives appropriate management authorization. Decision basis is objective evaluation against criteria, not resource limitations. Management must formally accept risks understanding potential consequences aligned with organizational risk appetite.
questions.justification_wrong: Budget constraints, peer practices, or resource limitations are insufficient justifications per ISO 27005:2022. Risk acceptance requires formal evaluation against criteria and explicit management authorization. Resource issues may necessitate alternative treatment options (transfer, avoidance) but don't automatically justify acceptance without criteria-based evaluation.

questions.text: According to ISO 27005:2022, which element must be defined FIRST before conducting a comprehensive information security risk assessment?
options: B. List of vulnerabilities affecting organizational assets
D. Existing security controls and their effectiveness levels
A. Risk acceptance criteria and risk evaluation criteria
C. Threat landscape and threat actor capabilities
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 clause 6.2 establishes that risk acceptance criteria and risk evaluation criteria must be defined before assessment activities. These criteria provide the baseline for comparing analyzed risks and making treatment decisions, forming the foundation of the risk assessment process per ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.1.2.
questions.justification_wrong: While vulnerabilities, threats, and controls are essential components, they are analyzed AFTER establishing the criteria framework. Without predefined acceptance and evaluation criteria, there is no reference point to determine risk significance or priority, violating the systematic approach required by both standards.

questions.text: During asset identification per ISO 27005:2022, an organization discovers a third-party cloud service containing critical customer data. How should this asset be classified in the asset inventory?
options: C. As a primary information asset only
B. As a supporting asset with associated dependencies documented
D. As an excluded asset due to third-party ownership
A. As an external asset outside the ISMS scope
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 clause 6.3.2 defines supporting assets as those that enable primary assets to exist or be processed. Cloud services are supporting assets that must be inventoried with dependencies mapped to primary assets (customer data). ISO 27001:2022 Annex A.5.9 requires supplier relationships to be addressed within scope.
questions.justification_wrong: Third-party services containing in-scope information cannot be excluded. They must be identified as supporting assets regardless of ownership. Failing to inventory and assess risks associated with cloud services creates gaps in the ISMS, violating ISO 27001:2022 clause 4.3 scope determination requirements.

questions.text: An organization identifies a vulnerability in its authentication system that could allow unauthorized access. According to ISO 27005:2022, what is the relationship between this vulnerability and potential threats?
options: C. Vulnerabilities and threats are synonymous terms in risk analysis
D. Threats must be present before vulnerabilities can be identified
B. Threats create vulnerabilities through repeated attack attempts
A. Vulnerabilities exist independently and threats exploit them to cause harm
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 clause 6.4.3 defines vulnerabilities as weaknesses that can be exploited by threats. Clause 6.4.2 defines threats as potential causes of incidents. The authentication weakness (vulnerability) exists independently, but becomes a risk only when threat actors or events could exploit it to cause unauthorized access impacts.
questions.justification_wrong: Threats do not create vulnerabilities; they exploit existing weaknesses. These are distinct concepts in risk analysis. Confusing them prevents proper risk identification and assessment, leading to inadequate control selection per ISO 27001:2022 Annex A. Vulnerabilities can exist without active threats but still require consideration.

questions.text: After completing risk evaluation, an organization must prepare a Risk Treatment Plan (RTP). Which element is MANDATORY in the RTP according to ISO 27001:2022?
options: D. Comparison with competitor security postures
A. Detailed cost-benefit analysis for each selected control
C. Third-party audit findings and remediation timelines
B. Risk owner assignments and resource requirements for implementation
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.1.3(e) requires the risk treatment plan to identify risk owners, actions, responsibilities, and resources. ISO 27005:2022 clause 6.5.3 emphasizes assignment of responsibilities and timelines. Without risk owners and resources, treatment actions cannot be effectively implemented or monitored per clause 6.2.
questions.justification_wrong: While cost-benefit analysis is valuable, it is not mandatory in the RTP. Third-party audits and competitor comparisons are not required elements. ISO 27001:2022 specifically mandates risk ownership, actions, responsibilities, and resources to ensure accountability and successful implementation of selected risk treatment options.

questions.text: An organization selects controls to reduce risk from 'High' to 'Medium', resulting in residual risk. According to ISO 27001:2022 and ISO 27005:2022, what is required regarding this residual risk?
options: A. Residual risk must be eliminated before ISMS certification
D. Residual risk should be transferred to cyber insurance providers
B. Residual risk must be accepted by risk owners with documented approval
C. Residual risk automatically becomes acceptable after control implementation
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.1.3(d) requires risk owners to accept residual risks and associated responsibilities. ISO 27005:2022 clause 6.5.2 mandates formal acceptance by appropriate management. Even after treatment, residual risks must be explicitly evaluated against acceptance criteria and formally accepted before proceeding with implementation.
questions.justification_wrong: Zero risk is impossible; residual risk always remains. Implementation does not automatically mean acceptance—formal approval is mandatory. While transfer is a treatment option, residual risk after any treatment requires explicit acceptance per clause 8.3. Certification does not require risk elimination, only proper treatment and acceptance.

questions.text: During risk identification, an organization discovers that customer payment data is stored in multiple locations including production servers, backup systems, and employee laptops. According to ISO 27005:2022, what is the PRIMARY reason for documenting all asset locations during the identification phase?
options: A. To ensure comprehensive risk assessment by identifying all points where threats could exploit vulnerabilities associated with the asset
B. To reduce the overall number of assets requiring protection through consolidation
D. To prioritize assets based solely on their physical location within the organization
C. To determine which department is financially responsible for asset protection costs
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 clause 6.2 emphasizes that asset identification must consider all instances and locations where information assets exist. Comprehensive identification ensures that risk assessment covers all threat-vulnerability combinations across all asset instances, preventing gaps in risk evaluation that could leave information exposed to unidentified risks.
questions.justification_wrong: Asset consolidation, cost allocation, and location-based prioritization are operational considerations but not the primary purpose of documenting asset locations during risk identification. The fundamental objective per ISO 27005 is ensuring complete risk coverage, not administrative efficiency or departmental accountability measures.

questions.text: An organization performing threat analysis identifies that a disgruntled employee with system administrator privileges could potentially delete critical databases. The likelihood is assessed as 'possible' and impact as 'severe'. What should be the NEXT step according to ISO 27005:2022 risk assessment methodology?
options: C. Calculate the residual risk assuming all controls fail completely
B. Assess existing vulnerabilities that could be exploited by this threat source to determine current security posture
D. Escalate to senior management for immediate personnel action
A. Immediately implement detective controls to monitor administrator activities
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 clause 6.4 specifies that after threat identification, vulnerability assessment must follow to determine which weaknesses could be exploited by identified threats. This systematic approach evaluates existing controls' effectiveness before determining risk levels, ensuring accurate risk estimation based on actual exposure rather than assumptions.
questions.justification_wrong: Implementing controls, calculating residual risk, or personnel actions are premature without completing vulnerability assessment. ISO 27005 requires methodical progression: identify threats, assess vulnerabilities, then estimate risk levels. Jumping to treatment or escalation violates the structured risk assessment process and may result in inappropriate resource allocation.

questions.text: During risk evaluation, an organization determines a risk level of 'Medium' for unauthorized access to financial records. The Risk Treatment Plan (RTP) proposes implementing multi-factor authentication (modify the risk). What must be included in the RTP according to ISO 27001:2022 Annex A control documentation requirements?
options: C. A comparison table showing all alternative controls that were considered and rejected
A. Only the control objective and the specific Annex A reference number
D. Detailed technical specifications of the authentication system vendor documentation
B. The control description, implementation timeline, responsible parties, and expected residual risk level
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.1.3(d) requires the risk treatment plan to document actions, responsibilities, and resources. ISO 27005:2022 clause 6.5.3 adds that RTPs must specify expected outcomes including residual risk. Comprehensive documentation ensures accountability, enables progress tracking, and validates that treatment achieves acceptable risk levels per organizational criteria.
questions.justification_wrong: While control objectives and references support documentation, they're insufficient alone. Alternative control comparisons and vendor specifications exceed RTP requirements and belong in supporting documentation. ISO standards require balanced documentation: sufficient detail for implementation accountability and risk validation, without excessive technical minutiae that obscures strategic risk treatment decisions.

questions.text: After implementing controls specified in the RTP, residual risk for data center physical access is calculated as 'Low'. However, this still exceeds the organization's risk acceptance criteria which only permits 'Very Low' risk for critical infrastructure. According to ISO 27001:2022, what is the REQUIRED next action?
options: D. Transfer the residual risk through insurance and accept the remaining exposure
C. Reclassify the data center as non-critical to align with current risk levels
B. Identify and implement additional risk treatment measures to reduce residual risk to acceptable levels
A. Document the risk acceptance decision with justification from top management and proceed with operations
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.1.3(e) explicitly requires that residual risks must obtain risk owner approval. When residual risk exceeds acceptance criteria, clause 6.2 mandates additional treatment to achieve acceptable levels. Organizations cannot proceed with non-conforming risks without proper risk acceptance authorization, which requires formal management approval demonstrating informed decision-making.
questions.justification_wrong: Risk acceptance requires formal top management approval when criteria aren't met, but additional treatment should be attempted first per ISO 27001 risk treatment hierarchy. Asset reclassification to circumvent criteria violates risk management principles and organizational integrity. Risk transfer alone rarely eliminates residual risk below acceptance thresholds without complementary treatment measures.

questions.text: An organization's risk assessment identifies that cloud service provider outages could impact business operations. The organization decides to accept this risk because mitigation costs exceed potential loss, and top management formally approves this decision. According to ISO 27005:2022, what is the MOST critical element to monitor during ongoing risk treatment?
options: C. The cloud provider's marketing materials regarding new service features
A. Changes in the threat landscape, vulnerabilities, or business context that could invalidate the original risk acceptance decision
D. Employee satisfaction scores regarding cloud service usability and performance
B. Competitor approaches to similar cloud service risks and their treatment choices
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 clause 7 (Risk monitoring and review) emphasizes that accepted risks must be continuously monitored for changes affecting validity of acceptance decisions. Changes in threats, vulnerabilities, asset value, or business impact could transform acceptable risks into unacceptable ones, requiring reassessment. ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.1.3 mandates periodic risk review ensuring continued appropriateness.
questions.justification_wrong: Competitor strategies, vendor marketing, and employee satisfaction provide contextual information but aren't critical monitoring elements for risk acceptance validity. ISO 27005 focuses monitoring on factors directly affecting risk calculations: threat evolution, vulnerability changes, asset criticality shifts, and impact variations that could necessitate risk treatment reassessment and potential acceptance decision reversal.

questions.text: During risk identification under ISO 27005:2022, an organization discovers that a critical asset supports multiple business processes with different confidentiality requirements. What is the MOST appropriate approach for asset valuation in this scenario?
options: B. Calculate an average value across all business processes to maintain consistency in the risk register
A. Assign the highest value based on the most critical business process and establish separate risk assessments for each context
C. Value the asset based solely on its replacement cost regardless of business context
D. Assign separate asset identifiers for each business process context with identical technical specifications
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 clause 6.3.2 emphasizes that asset valuation must consider business context and criticality. When an asset supports multiple processes with varying requirements, the highest consequence determines valuation, while separate assessments ensure appropriate controls for each context, maintaining risk assessment accuracy per clause 6.5.1.
questions.justification_wrong: Averaging values dilutes critical requirements and violates consequence-based valuation principles in ISO 27005:2022. Replacement cost alone ignores business impact. Creating duplicate technical assets causes inventory confusion and violates the principle of maintaining a coherent asset register per clause 6.3.2.

questions.text: An organization implementing ISO 27001:2022 identifies a vulnerability in legacy systems that cannot be patched. Threat actors are actively exploiting similar vulnerabilities globally. According to ISO 27005:2022, which factor is MOST critical when determining the likelihood of risk occurrence?
options: D. The average time between similar incidents reported in the organization's industry sector
C. The capability and motivation of relevant threat actors combined with the ease of vulnerability exploitation
A. The vulnerability severity score (CVSS) published by the vendor
B. Historical incident data from the organization's previous security breaches
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 clause 6.4.3 specifies that likelihood assessment must consider both threat source characteristics (capability, motivation, resources) and vulnerability exploitation factors. Active global exploitation indicates high threat motivation and proven capability, while unpatchable systems ensure exploitability, making this combination the primary likelihood determinant per clause 6.5.2.
questions.justification_wrong: CVSS scores assess technical severity but not actual threat landscape. Historical organizational data may not reflect current threat environments. Industry averages provide context but don't assess specific threat-vulnerability combinations. ISO 27005:2022 requires threat-centric likelihood assessment considering current threat intelligence and specific vulnerability characteristics per clause 6.4.3.

questions.text: During risk evaluation per ISO 27005:2022, an organization determines that a risk exceeds acceptance criteria but treatment costs exceed potential losses by 300%. Senior management proposes accepting the risk with enhanced monitoring. What is the CORRECT classification and requirement for this decision?
options: D. Conditional risk retention pending budget allocation in the next fiscal year without formal documentation
A. Risk acceptance requiring explicit documented approval from accountable risk owners and justification in the Risk Treatment Plan
C. Risk avoidance requiring immediate discontinuation of the vulnerable business process
B. Risk modification since monitoring represents a control implementation reducing the risk level
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 clause 6.7.2 and ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.1.3(e) mandate that accepting risks exceeding criteria requires explicit authorization from accountable parties with documented justification. Enhanced monitoring alone doesn't modify inherent risk. The Risk Treatment Plan must document this decision per ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.1.3, ensuring accountability and traceability.
questions.justification_wrong: Monitoring without risk modification is acceptance, not treatment per ISO 27005:2022 clause 6.7.3. Risk avoidance is disproportionate when costs exceed benefits significantly. Conditional retention without documentation violates ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.1.3 requirements for documented risk treatment decisions and explicit acceptance authorization per clause 6.1.3(e).

questions.text: An organization's Risk Treatment Plan addresses a high-priority risk through implementing multiple Annex A controls. After implementation, residual risk assessment shows the risk reduced but still above acceptance criteria. What is the MANDATORY next step according to ISO 27001:2022?
questions.tag: Risk Treatment Plan and Residual Risk
options: A. Document the residual risk status and obtain explicit acceptance authorization from risk owners before proceeding
B. Implement additional controls iteratively until the residual risk falls below acceptance criteria
C. Recalculate the risk acceptance criteria to align with the achieved residual risk level
D. Transfer the remaining risk to cyber insurance and document the policy in the Statement of Applicability
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.1.3(e) explicitly requires that residual risks must be accepted by risk owners with documented authorization when they cannot be reduced below acceptance criteria through reasonable controls. Clause 6.2 mandates documenting risk treatment outcomes. This ensures accountability and informed decision-making regarding remaining organizational risk exposure.
questions.justification_wrong: Continuous control implementation may be disproportionate or impractical per ISO 27005:2022 clause 6.7.1. Adjusting acceptance criteria to match residual risk undermines risk management integrity and violates ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.1.2 establishment principles. Insurance is risk sharing, not elimination, and still requires formal acceptance per clause 6.1.3(e).

questions.text: During residual risk assessment per ISO 27005:2022, an organization identifies that implementing compensating controls has created new vulnerabilities in adjacent systems. How should this scenario be addressed in the risk management process?
options: B. Document the new vulnerabilities as acceptable trade-offs in the residual risk register without further assessment
D. Classify the new vulnerabilities as implementation errors requiring correction before completing the residual risk assessment
C. Reverse the compensating controls implementation and restore the original system configuration
A. Initiate a new risk assessment iteration treating the newly identified vulnerabilities as emerging risks and update the Risk Treatment Plan accordingly
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 clause 8.4 emphasizes continuous monitoring and reassessment when circumstances change. Clause 6.7.1 acknowledges that risk treatment may introduce new risks requiring assessment. ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.1.3 requires iterative risk assessment processes. New vulnerabilities must be formally assessed and addressed through updated risk treatment per clause 6.3.
questions.justification_wrong: Documenting without assessment violates ISO 27005:2022 clause 6.5 requirements for systematic risk evaluation. Reversing controls may restore original high risks without solving the problem. Classifying as errors oversimplifies the complexity where legitimate controls create secondary effects. ISO 27005:2022 clause 8.4 requires continuous improvement through reassessment.

questions.text: An organization identified a critical asset storing personal health information. During risk assessment, a ransomware threat with HIGH likelihood and VERY HIGH impact was identified. The risk owner proposes encrypting backups stored on the same network. What is the PRIMARY deficiency of this treatment approach per ISO 27005:2022?
options: C. Encryption of backups is a detective control rather than a preventive control for ransomware threats
B. The treatment violates ISO 27001:2022 Annex A.8.13 requirements for information backup procedures
D. The treatment plan lacks quantitative metrics for measuring residual risk reduction effectiveness
A. The treatment does not adequately reduce vulnerability; encrypted backups on the same network remain susceptible to the same ransomware attack vector
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 clause 6.4.3 requires risk treatment to effectively modify risk. Network-connected encrypted backups fail to address the attack vector vulnerability. Ransomware can encrypt already-encrypted backups or delete them through network access. Effective treatment requires offline/air-gapped backups per ISO 27002:2022 control 8.13, addressing the vulnerability itself.
questions.justification_wrong: While control implementation and metrics are important, the fundamental flaw is inadequate vulnerability reduction. The treatment appears to address confidentiality but ignores availability impact. ISO 27005:2022 emphasizes treatments must effectively reduce likelihood or consequence. Network-accessible backups remain vulnerable regardless of encryption status, leaving primary attack path unaddressed.

questions.text: During information security risk assessment per ISO 27005:2022, an organization established risk criteria including risk acceptance thresholds. The CISO wants to accept a HIGH risk (score 18/25) affecting customer data availability because mitigation costs exceed annual revenue from affected service. What is the MOST critical consideration before accepting this risk?
options: D. Whether the risk score calculation methodology accounts for both single loss expectancy and annual rate of occurrence
C. Whether alternative risk treatment options including risk sharing through cyber insurance have been evaluated and documented
A. Whether the risk acceptance is properly authorized by management with accountability for the consequences and documented per ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.1.3
B. Whether the organization has consulted legal counsel regarding regulatory compliance obligations under applicable data protection laws
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 clause 6.4.4 requires considering legal, regulatory, and contractual obligations before risk acceptance. ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.1.3(e) mandates retained risks meet legal requirements. Data protection laws often prohibit accepting availability risks for personal data regardless of economic justification. Regulatory penalties and customer rights supersede cost-benefit analysis.
questions.justification_wrong: While management authorization (6.1.3), treatment alternatives (6.4.3), and calculation methodology (6.3) are important ISO 27005 requirements, they become secondary when legal obligations exist. Organizations cannot accept risks that violate mandatory regulatory requirements regardless of authorization level, alternative options, or quantitative justification. Compliance obligations establish absolute constraints on risk acceptance decisions.

questions.text: An asset owner classified a database server as having HIGH confidentiality, MEDIUM integrity, and HIGH availability requirements. During threat analysis per ISO 27005:2022, a SQL injection vulnerability (CVSSv3: 8.6) was identified. The IT department argues this is a LOW risk because the application has input validation. What critical error exists in this risk evaluation?
options: D. The risk assessment should use organizational risk criteria rather than external CVSS scores to determine risk level classifications
C. SQL injection primarily threatens data integrity rather than confidentiality, creating a mismatch between vulnerability type and asset classification priorities
A. Input validation is a compensating control that should reduce likelihood but cannot change the inherent vulnerability rating or potential impact on asset confidentiality
B. The evaluation confuses vulnerability severity (CVSS score) with risk level, failing to properly assess both likelihood and consequence per ISO 27005 methodology
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 clause 6.3 defines risk as combination of consequence and likelihood. CVSS measures vulnerability severity, not risk. The error is conflating vulnerability rating with risk level without proper likelihood and impact analysis. Input validation may reduce exploitation likelihood but doesn't eliminate the vulnerability. Risk assessment requires evaluating both dimensions per established organizational criteria.
questions.justification_wrong: While controls affect likelihood and organizational criteria are important, the fundamental error is methodological confusion between vulnerability severity and risk. CVSS provides technical severity; risk assessment must separately evaluate exploitation likelihood and business impact. The presence of controls should be factored into likelihood assessment, not used to arbitrarily override vulnerability severity or dismiss proper risk analysis.

questions.text: Following risk treatment implementation, an organization calculated residual risk for unauthorized access to financial systems as MEDIUM (reduced from HIGH). The Risk Treatment Plan specified quarterly reviews. Six months later, new threat intelligence reveals active exploitation of a related vulnerability. Per ISO 27005:2022, what is the MANDATORY next action?
options: D. Update the risk register to reclassify the residual risk as HIGH and implement compensating detective controls pending the quarterly review
A. Immediately escalate to senior management for emergency risk acceptance decision since the RTP review cycle has not been completed
C. Document the threat intelligence as part of the next scheduled quarterly review and continue monitoring for actual exploitation incidents
B. Initiate an unscheduled risk assessment to re-evaluate the risk level considering the changed threat landscape and determine if additional treatment is required
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 clause 6.6 requires monitoring and review when significant changes occur in threat environment. Clause 6.2 emphasizes continuous risk identification. Active exploitation represents material change requiring immediate reassessment regardless of scheduled review cycles. ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.1.1(d) mandates updating risk assessment when necessary. Threat landscape changes trigger unscheduled reviews to maintain risk treatment effectiveness.
questions.justification_wrong: Waiting for scheduled reviews (C) contradicts ISO 27005 principles of continuous monitoring and timely response. Arbitrary reclassification without assessment (D) lacks analytical rigor. Immediate escalation (A) presumes outcome without analysis. ISO 27005 emphasizes systematic reassessment when circumstances change. Organizations must evaluate whether existing treatments remain effective against evolved threats before determining appropriate management responses.

questions.text: An organization performs risk assessment using asset-based methodology per ISO 27005:2022. For a cloud-hosted CRM system, they identified the cloud provider as an asset and assessed risks including provider bankruptcy. The information security manager challenges this approach. What is the PRIMARY methodological concern with this asset identification?
options: A. Cloud service providers should be identified as external dependencies or supporting assets rather than primary information assets requiring protection
B. Provider bankruptcy is a business continuity risk outside the scope of ISO 27001:2022 information security risk assessment
C. Third-party risks should be addressed through contractual controls in Annex A.5.19 rather than organizational risk assessment processes
D. Cloud services represent shared responsibility models requiring separate risk assessment frameworks incompatible with ISO 27005 methodology
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 clause 6.2.2.2 distinguishes primary assets (information/processes) from supporting assets (infrastructure/services). Cloud providers are supporting assets or external dependencies, not information assets themselves. The CRM data is the asset requiring protection. Provider-related risks should be assessed as threats/vulnerabilities affecting primary asset availability, not treating the provider as an asset itself, ensuring proper risk-asset relationship.
questions.justification_wrong: Provider bankruptcy is relevant to information availability (not outside scope). Contractual controls complement but don't replace risk assessment requirements. ISO 27005 fully accommodates cloud and shared responsibility models. The core issue is conceptual: confusing supporting infrastructure with information assets. This misidentification distorts risk relationships, potentially missing actual information security risks while focusing on business relationship risks better addressed through supply chain management frameworks.

questions.text: An organization has identified a critical database server containing personal data. During asset identification per ISO 27001:2022 and ISO 27005:2022, which attribute is MOST essential to document for subsequent risk assessment phases?
options: D. Power consumption, cooling requirements, rack position, and network segment
B. Physical location, purchase date, warranty information, and vendor contact details
C. Operating system version, patch level, backup schedule, and monitoring tools
A. Asset owner, asset value, dependencies, and information classification level
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 Clause 6.3.2 requires identification of asset ownership, value, classification, and dependencies. These attributes directly support threat-vulnerability pairing and consequence analysis. Asset owner accountability and value assessment are fundamental for determining appropriate controls and risk treatment priorities per ISO 27001:2022 Clause 6.1.2.
questions.justification_wrong: Options B, C, and D represent operational or technical attributes useful for asset management but insufficient for risk assessment. ISO 27005:2022 emphasizes information security properties—confidentiality, integrity, availability—requiring owner identification and classification rather than purely technical specifications or facilities management data for effective risk evaluation.

questions.text: During threat analysis, a financial institution identifies 'SQL injection attacks' as a relevant threat to their web application. Which vulnerability assessment approach aligns BEST with ISO 27005:2022 methodology?
options: D. Assessing the likelihood of threat occurrence without examining control effectiveness
C. Documenting all theoretical vulnerabilities from CVE databases without contextual validation
B. Reviewing the threat actor's motivation, capability, and historical attack patterns only
A. Examining application code review results, penetration test findings, and input validation controls to identify exploitable weaknesses
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 Clause 6.4.3 requires vulnerability identification relating to specific threats and existing controls. Option A correctly examines actual weaknesses (code flaws, validation gaps) that the identified threat could exploit, considering control effectiveness. This systematic approach enables accurate threat-vulnerability pairing essential for consequence and likelihood determination per Clause 6.5.
questions.justification_wrong: Option B focuses solely on threat analysis without vulnerability assessment. Option C generates noise through non-contextual data. Option D reverses the proper sequence—likelihood assessment requires understanding both vulnerabilities and existing controls. ISO 27005:2022 mandates identifying exploitable weaknesses specific to identified threats before likelihood estimation.

questions.text: An organization completes risk evaluation using a 5×5 matrix (consequence × likelihood). Three risks score 'High' (15-25). Management capacity allows treating only two risks immediately. What is the PRIMARY criterion for prioritization per ISO 27001:2022?
options: C. Prioritization based solely on numerical risk scores without contextual analysis
D. Focus exclusively on risks identified by external auditors or certification bodies
A. Alignment with organizational risk acceptance criteria, business objectives, and legal/regulatory obligations
B. Selection of risks with the lowest treatment costs to maximize budget efficiency
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 6.1.3(c) requires risk evaluation against risk acceptance criteria considering legal, regulatory, and contractual obligations. ISO 27005:2022 Clause 6.6.3 emphasizes business context, compliance requirements, and stakeholder concerns. Prioritization must reflect organizational objectives and mandatory requirements, not merely numerical scores or cost considerations alone.
questions.justification_wrong: Option B prioritizes cost over risk significance, violating risk-based approach principles. Option C ignores contextual factors required by ISO 27001:2022 Clause 6.1.2. Option D inappropriately elevates external perspectives over organizational risk ownership. ISO 27005:2022 requires decisions based on comprehensive evaluation including compliance, business impact, and strategic alignment.

questions.text: After risk assessment, a manufacturing company selects 'risk modification' for a high-risk scenario involving industrial control system compromise. Which action demonstrates CORRECT implementation of this treatment option per ISO 27005:2022?
options: D. Discontinuing all industrial control system operations and reverting to manual processes
C. Documenting acceptance of the risk in the Risk Treatment Plan without further action
A. Implementing network segmentation, multi-factor authentication, and intrusion detection to reduce likelihood and consequence
B. Purchasing cyber insurance coverage without implementing additional technical or organizational controls
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 Clause 6.7.3.2 defines risk modification as applying controls to reduce risk likelihood, consequence, or both. Option A correctly implements multiple Annex A controls (network security, access control, monitoring) addressing both dimensions. This aligns with ISO 27001:2022 Clause 6.1.3(d) requiring appropriate control selection for identified risks.
questions.justification_wrong: Option B represents risk sharing (transfer), not modification—ISO 27005:2022 Clause 6.7.3.4. Option C represents risk retention (acceptance)—Clause 6.7.3.5. Option D represents risk avoidance—Clause 6.7.3.3. Only risk modification involves implementing controls to reduce risk levels while maintaining business activities, as required by the scenario.

questions.text: An organization's Risk Treatment Plan (RTP) addresses 47 identified risks. Senior management requests evidence that residual risks are within acceptable levels. What documentation MUST be provided per ISO 27001:2022 requirements?
options: D. Insurance policies covering all identified risks with premium payment receipts
B. Original risk assessment report only, without post-treatment evaluation or acceptance records
A. Documented residual risk assessment results, formal acceptance by risk owners, and Statement of Applicability alignment
C. External auditor certification confirming all residual risks are eliminated completely
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 6.1.3(e) explicitly requires residual risk acceptance by risk owners and retention of documented information on risk assessment results. Clause 6.1.3(c) requires demonstrating residual risks meet acceptance criteria. The SoA per Clause 6.1.3(d) must reflect controls addressing risks, enabling verification that residual levels are acceptable and appropriately managed.
questions.justification_wrong: Option B lacks post-treatment evaluation required by ISO 27001:2022 Clause 8.1. Option C misrepresents reality—risk elimination is rarely achievable or cost-effective; ISO 27005:2022 acknowledges residual risk existence. Option D confuses risk sharing mechanisms with acceptance documentation. Formal risk owner acceptance and residual risk evaluation are mandatory documented information requirements.

questions.text: During a risk assessment for a financial institution, you identify that customer transaction data is stored across three different systems with varying security controls. According to ISO 27005:2022, what is the MOST appropriate approach to asset identification in this scenario?
options: C. Identify only the most critical system as an asset since the others support it and will inherit the same risk treatment
A. Identify each system as a separate primary asset and document their interdependencies, information flows, and aggregate valuation based on confidentiality, integrity, and availability requirements
B. Consolidate all three systems into a single asset entry to simplify the risk assessment process and apply uniform controls
D. Focus exclusively on the data itself as the primary asset without considering the supporting systems as separate assets
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 requires comprehensive asset identification including information assets and supporting assets with documented relationships. Each system must be identified separately as supporting assets with their interdependencies mapped to understand the complete risk landscape and apply appropriate, differentiated controls based on individual vulnerabilities.
questions.justification_wrong: Consolidating assets oversimplifies risk assessment and violates ISO 27005 principles requiring detailed asset inventories. Focusing only on one system or just data ignores supporting assets and their unique vulnerabilities. ISO 27001:2022 Annex A.5.9 requires complete asset inventories with ownership and classification.

questions.text: An organization has completed threat and vulnerability analysis for its cloud infrastructure. A vulnerability exists in the encryption protocol (CVSSv3 score 7.8), but the threat source requires nation-state capabilities. The likelihood of exploitation is assessed as 'rare'. What should the risk assessment conclude per ISO 27005:2022?
options: B. Discount the vulnerability entirely since nation-state actors are outside the organization's threat model
D. Apply the precautionary principle and treat all vulnerabilities with CVSS scores above 7.0 as critical risks
A. The risk is automatically high due to the CVSS score and must be treated immediately regardless of threat likelihood
C. The risk level depends on both the vulnerability severity and realistic threat likelihood; the combination determines if the risk is acceptable within the organization's risk acceptance criteria
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 clause 6.4 establishes that risk is determined by combining consequence and likelihood. Both vulnerability severity and realistic threat likelihood must be considered together. The organization's established risk acceptance criteria determine treatment priorities, not vulnerability scores alone. Context-specific assessment is mandatory per ISO 27005.
questions.justification_wrong: CVSS scores indicate vulnerability severity but don't determine risk level independently per ISO 27005. Ignoring vulnerabilities violates defense-in-depth principles. Automatic treatment based solely on CVSS contradicts ISO 27005's requirement for contextual risk evaluation considering both likelihood and impact within organizational risk appetite.

questions.text: During risk evaluation, you identify a risk with likelihood 'possible' and impact 'major'. The organization's risk acceptance criteria shows this falls in the 'medium' zone. Management proposes accepting this risk without treatment. As Lead Implementer, what is your appropriate response according to ISO 27001:2022?
options: A. Document management's decision in the Risk Treatment Plan with explicit acknowledgment that medium-level risks can only be accepted with top management authorization and documented justification per clause 6.1.3(e)
C. Accept the decision without additional requirements since the risk falls within defined acceptable levels
D. Require a third-party audit opinion before allowing acceptance of any medium-level risk
B. Reject the decision as ISO 27001 mandates that all medium and high risks must be treated and cannot be accepted
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.1.3(e) requires retaining documented information on the risk assessment process including risk treatment decisions. Clause 6.2 requires top management ownership. Medium risks may be accepted if authorized by appropriate authority with documented justification, ensuring accountability and informed decision-making per ISO 27005:2022 clause 8.3.
questions.justification_wrong: ISO 27001 doesn't mandate treatment of all medium risks; risk acceptance is permitted with proper authorization. Automatic acceptance without documentation violates clause 6.1.3 requirements. Third-party audits aren't mandatory for risk acceptance decisions, though governance and accountability through documented management decisions are required.

questions.text: Your organization selects 'risk modification' as treatment for a high risk related to third-party data processing. You must define controls from ISO 27001:2022 Annex A. Which combination BEST demonstrates comprehensive risk treatment for supplier relationships per ISO 27005:2022?
options: C. A.8.1 (user endpoint devices) and A.8.2 (privileged access rights) to control supplier access points
D. A.6.1 (screening) and A.6.2 (terms of employment) applied to supplier personnel
B. A.5.19 (supplier relationships security) only, as it comprehensively covers all supplier-related controls
A. A.5.19 (supplier relationships security), A.5.20 (addressing security in supplier agreements), A.5.21 (managing security in ICT supply chain), with defined monitoring and review mechanisms
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Annex A controls 5.19, 5.20, and 5.21 specifically address comprehensive supplier security management including relationship establishment, contractual agreements, and supply chain security. ISO 27005:2022 clause 8.3.2 requires controls to be proportionate and comprehensive. This combination addresses governance, contractual, and operational aspects of supplier risk.
questions.justification_wrong: Single controls provide insufficient coverage for complex third-party risks. Access controls (A.8.1, A.8.2) address only technical aspects, ignoring contractual and governance elements. HR controls (A.6.1, A.6.2) don't apply to external supplier organizations. ISO 27005 requires layered, comprehensive control selection for risk modification.

questions.text: After implementing risk treatment controls, you calculate residual risk. The residual risk for a critical asset remains in the 'medium' range. The Risk Treatment Plan (RTP) shows this was anticipated. What is the REQUIRED next step per ISO 27001:2022 and ISO 27005:2022?
options: A. Obtain formal acceptance of residual risk from risk owners and top management, document the decision with justification, and ensure it's included in the documented ISMS scope per clauses 6.1.3(e) and 6.2
D. Transfer the residual risk through insurance which automatically satisfies ISO 27001 requirements for residual risk management
C. Document the residual risk in the RTP without requiring formal acceptance since it was anticipated during planning
B. Immediately implement additional controls until residual risk is reduced to 'low' level regardless of cost-benefit analysis
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.1.3(e) requires retaining documented information on risk treatment results. ISO 27005:2022 clause 8.4 mandates that residual risks must be formally accepted by risk owners with appropriate authority. Top management approval per clause 6.2 ensures accountability. Documentation in the ISMS demonstrates compliance with systematic risk management.
questions.justification_wrong: Mandatory reduction to 'low' contradicts risk-based thinking; residual risk acceptance is legitimate per ISO 27005. Anticipated residual risk still requires formal acceptance documentation per clause 6.1.3. Insurance transfers financial consequences but doesn't eliminate acceptance requirements or management responsibility for information security risks.

questions.text: During risk identification according to ISO 27005:2022, an organization discovers that several information assets share interdependencies that could amplify consequences during incidents. What is the MOST appropriate action to address this in the risk assessment process?
options: D. Establish separate risk treatment plans for each asset without considering their relationships or combined impact scenarios
A. Document asset interdependencies and evaluate aggregated consequences during risk analysis to reflect cascading impacts on confidentiality, integrity, and availability
B. Treat each asset independently to simplify the risk assessment and avoid complexity in consequence evaluation
C. Only consider the asset with the highest individual value and disregard interdependencies to focus resources efficiently
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 Clause 6.5.2 emphasizes identifying relationships between assets as dependencies can significantly affect risk levels. Aggregated consequence evaluation ensures accurate risk assessment reflecting realistic scenarios where cascading failures occur, supporting comprehensive treatment strategies aligned with organizational context.
questions.justification_wrong: Independent assessment ignores critical interdependencies that can exponentially increase actual risk exposure. ISO 27005:2022 requires holistic analysis considering asset relationships and their combined vulnerability to threats. Isolated evaluation underestimates organizational risk, violating risk assessment principles requiring context-aware analysis of information asset ecosystems.

questions.text: An organization has completed threat and vulnerability analysis per ISO 27005:2022 and identified a vulnerability with no currently known threats exploiting it. How should this be addressed in the risk evaluation phase?
options: A. Exclude this vulnerability from risk evaluation since no active threats exist, focusing only on vulnerabilities with identified threat actors
D. Transfer the vulnerability documentation to external auditors without internal risk treatment consideration
C. Immediately implement maximum security controls to eliminate the vulnerability regardless of threat landscape or resource constraints
B. Document the vulnerability and assign a nominal risk level, monitoring for emerging threats as part of continuous risk assessment activities
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 Clause 6.6.2 requires comprehensive vulnerability documentation even without current threats. Risk landscapes evolve; dormant vulnerabilities can become critical when new threats emerge. Continuous monitoring and periodic reassessment ensure organizational preparedness, aligning with dynamic risk management principles mandated by ISO 27001:2022 Clause 6.1.3.
questions.justification_wrong: Excluding documented vulnerabilities violates ISO 27005:2022 principles of comprehensive risk identification. Threat landscapes are dynamic; today's unthreatened vulnerability becomes tomorrow's critical exposure. Proper risk management requires documenting all vulnerabilities, assigning appropriate monitoring procedures, and maintaining readiness for emerging threats through continuous assessment frameworks.

questions.text: During risk treatment planning per ISO 27005:2022, management proposes accepting a high-level risk without implementing controls due to budget constraints. What is the ESSENTIAL requirement before this decision can be formally approved?
options: B. Simply document the decision in the risk register without requiring additional management approval or justification processes
A. Obtain explicit approval from top management with documented justification, ensuring the decision aligns with organizational risk acceptance criteria and legal obligations
D. Defer the decision until budget becomes available, leaving the risk unaddressed in the interim period
C. Automatically implement insurance transfer mechanisms instead of formal acceptance procedures to maintain compliance status
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 6.1.3(e) explicitly requires organizational risk acceptance criteria and risk acceptance decisions to be made by appropriate authority. ISO 27005:2022 Clause 6.8.3 mandates documented justification and top management approval for risk acceptance, particularly for high-level risks, ensuring accountability and informed decision-making aligned with organizational objectives.
questions.justification_wrong: Risk acceptance without proper authorization violates ISO 27001:2022 mandatory requirements. Clause 6.1.3 demands formal approval processes, documented criteria, and management accountability. Undocumented acceptance or procedural shortcuts create compliance gaps, potential liability issues, and undermine the ISMS governance structure. All risk acceptance decisions require explicit management endorsement and justification.

questions.text: An organization has implemented risk treatment controls and must now evaluate residual risk per ISO 27005:2022. The residual risk exceeds organizational acceptance criteria. What is the MOST appropriate next step according to the standard?
options: B. Initiate additional risk treatment iteration, selecting supplementary controls or treatment options until residual risk meets acceptance criteria
A. Document the residual risk and proceed to ISMS operation since controls have been implemented and resources exhausted
C. Automatically escalate to risk acceptance procedures regardless of whether alternative treatment options have been fully explored
D. Reclassify the risk level to fit within acceptance criteria without implementing additional controls or obtaining management approval
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 Clause 6.8.4 states residual risk exceeding acceptance criteria requires further treatment. The risk treatment process is iterative; additional controls must be considered until residual risk becomes acceptable or formal acceptance is obtained. This ensures systematic risk reduction aligned with ISO 27001:2022 Clause 6.1.3 requirements for managing information security risks.
questions.justification_wrong: Proceeding with unacceptable residual risk without proper treatment or formal acceptance violates ISO 27005:2022 core principles. Organizations must either reduce risk through additional controls or obtain explicit management acceptance per defined criteria. Arbitrary reclassification or documentation without action undermines ISMS integrity and creates compliance failures against ISO 27001:2022 requirements.

questions.text: According to ISO 27005:2022, when developing the Risk Treatment Plan (RTP), which element is MANDATORY to ensure effective implementation and alignment with ISO 27001:2022 requirements?
options: C. External consultant validation of all treatment decisions before internal management approval and resource allocation
D. Complete elimination of all identified risks through preventive controls regardless of organizational risk appetite
B. Assignment of responsibilities, timelines, resources, and expected outcomes for each risk treatment action with measurable criteria
A. Detailed cost-benefit analysis for each proposed control with ROI calculations and financial approval workflows
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 Clause 6.8.2 requires the RTP to specify actions, responsibilities, timelines, and resources. ISO 27001:2022 Clause 6.1.3(d) mandates defining how to treat risks with clear implementation plans. Measurable criteria enable monitoring and verification, ensuring accountability and effective risk management integration within the ISMS framework.
questions.justification_wrong: While cost considerations are important, ISO 27005:2022 does not mandate detailed ROI calculations in the RTP. External validation is not required; internal governance suffices. Complete risk elimination is unrealistic and contradicts risk-based approaches. The mandatory elements are responsibility assignment, timelines, resources, and measurable outcomes for systematic implementation and compliance.

questions.text: An organization performing risk assessment per ISO 27005:2022 identifies a critical asset processing personal data. The asset owner estimates likelihood as 'Medium' and impact as 'High'. Which action demonstrates proper application of risk treatment according to ISO 27001:2022 Clause 6.1.3?
options: B. Immediately implement all Annex A controls related to personal data protection without further analysis
D. Accept the risk by default since it falls within the medium-high range of the risk matrix
C. Transfer the risk entirely to a third-party processor and close the risk treatment activity
A. Document the risk treatment plan with selected controls, assign responsibilities, allocate resources, and define timelines for implementation
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 6.1.3(e) requires formulating risk treatment plans including information security controls, resources, responsibilities, and timelines. ISO 27005:2022 Section 8.3 emphasizes documenting treatment decisions with implementation details. This systematic approach ensures accountability and traceability.
questions.justification_wrong: Option B violates proportionality principles and risk-based thinking. Option C oversimplifies risk transfer without proper due diligence. Option D misapplies risk acceptance without proper authorization. All violate ISO 27001:2022 requirements for deliberate, documented risk treatment decisions per Clause 6.1.3.

questions.text: During vulnerability assessment using ISO 27005:2022 methodology, multiple vulnerabilities are identified for a single asset. The lead implementer must prioritize analysis. Which approach aligns with ISO 27005:2022 Section 7.3 for vulnerability evaluation?
options: A. Analyze vulnerabilities based on technical severity scores from CVE databases only
B. Assess vulnerabilities considering ease of exploitation, existing controls, and threat-vulnerability pairing relevance
D. Focus exclusively on vulnerabilities with publicly available exploit code
C. Prioritize all critical-rated vulnerabilities equally regardless of asset context or threat landscape
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 Section 7.3 requires vulnerability assessment to consider technical attributes, exploitability, effectiveness of existing controls, and relevance to identified threats. This contextual analysis ensures realistic risk evaluation rather than generic scoring, supporting accurate likelihood determination per Section 7.4.2.
questions.justification_wrong: Option A ignores organizational context and existing controls. Option C fails to consider actual exploitability and threat-vulnerability relationships. Option D creates artificial limitations contradicting comprehensive vulnerability assessment requirements. ISO 27005:2022 demands context-aware analysis considering multiple factors beyond technical ratings alone.

questions.text: An organization establishes its Risk Treatment Plan (RTP) after completing risk assessment. Senior management questions why residual risks exceed risk acceptance criteria for two identified risks. What does ISO 27001:2022 require in this scenario?
options: C. Implement additional controls immediately without management approval to reduce residual risk
B. Automatically reject the Risk Treatment Plan and restart the entire risk assessment process
A. Obtain explicit acceptance from risk owners and senior management for residual risks exceeding criteria, documented per Clause 6.1.3(e)
D. Reclassify the risk acceptance criteria to accommodate the identified residual risks
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 6.1.3(e) explicitly requires obtaining risk owners' acceptance of residual risks and acceptance of the plan to treat information security risks. Clause 6.2 requires documented information. ISO 27005:2022 Section 8.4.4 reinforces that residual risks exceeding acceptance levels require explicit senior management approval.
questions.justification_wrong: Option B is unnecessarily disruptive and not required by standards. Option C violates governance principles requiring management authorization per Clause 5.1. Option D represents manipulation of criteria post-assessment, undermining risk management integrity. ISO 27001:2022 mandates explicit acceptance through proper authorization channels.

questions.text: During asset identification per ISO 27005:2022 Section 7.2.2, the implementation team debates whether employee knowledge and competencies should be classified as information assets. What principle from ISO 27001:2022 supports the correct classification approach?
options: C. Classify only documented knowledge; exclude tacit employee knowledge from asset register
D. Categorize separately as human resources, outside information security scope
A. Classify as information assets since organizational knowledge directly impacts information security objectives and requires protection
B. Exclude from asset inventory as intangible assets cannot be adequately risk-assessed
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 Section 7.2.2 includes 'information and knowledge' as asset examples. ISO 27001:2022 Clause 7.2 emphasizes competence and 4.1 requires understanding organizational knowledge. Employee competencies and organizational knowledge directly affect information security effectiveness, warranting protection as information assets within ISMS scope.
questions.justification_wrong: Option B contradicts ISO 27005:2022 which explicitly includes intangible assets. Option C artificially limits asset scope, creating protection gaps. Option D misunderstands ISMS scope; ISO 27001:2022 Clause 4.3 requires considering all relevant internal issues, including human capital affecting information security objectives and capabilities.

questions.text: An organization completes threat identification per ISO 27005:2022 and identifies 47 distinct threats across its asset inventory. The lead implementer must ensure comprehensive threat-vulnerability analysis. Which approach best satisfies ISO 27005:2022 Section 7.4 requirements for likelihood determination?
options: C. Prioritize threats exclusively by historical incident frequency within the organization
A. Assess threat likelihood by evaluating threat source motivation, capability, opportunity, and vulnerability exploitability for each asset-threat pair
B. Apply uniform probability values to threat categories based on industry statistics only
D. Use threat intelligence feeds as sole determinant for likelihood ratings
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005:2022 Section 7.4.2 specifies likelihood determination must consider threat source attributes (motivation, capability), opportunity, and vulnerability attractiveness/exploitability. This multi-factor analysis per Section 7.2.4 ensures realistic likelihood estimation reflecting actual organizational context rather than generic statistical approaches.
questions.justification_wrong: Option B ignores organization-specific context contradicting ISO 27005:2022 context establishment requirements. Option C creates recency bias, missing emerging threats. Option D over-relies on external data without organizational context. Section 7.4.2 requires balanced consideration of multiple factors including threat-specific and vulnerability-specific attributes together.
